description: A psychology instructor needs to analyze the Serial Position Effect from Free Recall memory data located at ~/pebl/data/freerecall_data.csv (columns: participant_id, list_index, study_position, word, recalled, and recall_rt_ms). Analyze the data for 30 students, excluding any invalid entries where overall recall is 0.0 and mean reaction time is less than 50ms. For each valid participant, calculate the mean recall probability for primacy (positions 1-3), middle (positions 4-12), recency (positions 13-15), and overall recall. Generate a group serial position curve representing the grand mean recall probability for each of the 15 study positions across all valid participants. Export the results to ~/pebl/analysis/serial_position_report.json. The output must contain a 'participants' list (including 'id', 'primacy_score', 'middle_score', 'recency_score', 'overall_recall', and exclusion status/reason) and a 'group_serial_position_curve' array of 15 floats.

metadata.data_source: Programmatically generated unique dataset simulating human free recall with serial position effects
